steps.title: Compare the study settings
steps.content: The captive chimpanzees and squirrel monkeys showed more right-handedness, while the wild orangutans did not. The passage then highlights a key difference: captive animals spend much more time with humans, most of whom are right-handed.

steps.title: Use the contact explanation
steps.content: A and D question sample size, and B points to a possible difference in measurement standards. Those explanations do not use the passage's emphasis on human contact. The logical completion attributes the captive animals' right-handedness partly to extended contact with humans. The answer is C.
